ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-07-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Beth Kramer appeared by zoom for Sean Williams and Edward Maher (Co-Conservators).

The Court questions how a $300,000.00 business generated a $150,000.00 tax liability. Ms. Kramer stated that the Decedent owned a piece of property in Mexico through several layers of entities, but the ultimate owner was the IRA, spoke of the arista statute and the issue of self-dealings, and stated that they were advised that the best thing to do is to terminate the IRA. Ms. Kramer also stated that Chop Chop Hacienda LLC was a personal property, that the reserve is to pay the $150,000.00 tax liability and there is concern the IRS could come back with a penalty, and that she would normally wait three years to distribute the reserve, but if they get closing communication from the IRS, they could distribute it/close sooner. The First and Final Report of Administrators on Waiver of Accounting, etc. is under submission. Status on distribution of the reserve is set for June 29, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Kramer is to file an informal accounting of the reserve one week prior.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.
